mission.title: consul: New WARN in 1.10.0 caused by shuffling the servers in the gRPC ClientConn pool
mission.description: New WARN in 1.10.0 caused by shuffling the servers in the gRPC ClientConn pool. Users encounter: "desc = "transport: Error while dialing dial tcp 10.200.65.16:8300: operation was canceled". Reconnec".

steps.title: Identify consul troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your consul deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul -n consul --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `desc = "transport: Error while dialing dial tcp 10.200.65.16:8300: operation was canceled". Reconnecting...``

steps.title: Review consul configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
**Note from :** I'm moving this to hashicorp/consul because the discuss post shows a user on EC2 also saw this error.

steps.title: Apply the fix for New WARN in 1.10.0 caused by shuffling the servers in the…
steps.description:
Users are reporting frequently logged WARNs which are being output by gRPC.
They are caused by our gRPC resolver attempting to shuffle server addresses by:
1. Randomly shuffle server addresses every T interval
2. Pass the first address to update the gRPC client connection which kills any existing
```yaml
client:
  enabled: true
  grpc: true
connectInject:
  aclBindingRuleSelector: serviceaccount.name!=default
  default: false
  enabled: true
  metrics:
    defaultEnableMerging: true
    defaultEnabled: true
    defaultMergedMetricsPort: 20100
    defaultPrometheusScrapePath: /metrics
    defaultPrometheusScrapePort: 20200
  transparentProxy:
    defaultEnabled: true
    defaultOverwriteProbes: true
controller:
  enabled: true
dns:
  enabled: true
global:
  acls:
    createReplicationToken: true
    manageSystemACLs: true
  datacenter: primary
  enabled: true
  federation:
    createFederationS
```

steps.title: Confirm New WARN in 1.10.0 caused by shuffling the… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul -n consul --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `desc = "transport: Error while dialing dial tcp 10.200.65.16:8300: operation was canceled". Reconnecting...`` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
Users are reporting frequently logged WARNs which are being output by gRPC.
They are caused by our gRPC resolver attempting to shuffle server addresses by:
1. Randomly shuffle server addresses every T interval
2. Pass the first address to update the gRPC client connection which kills any existing connections (unless the first address happens to be equal to the existing)
3.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with consul installed or the issue environment reproducible.
